title: DIY Dog Crate Console
summary: This DIY Dog Crate Console is the perfect combination of form and function.
description: This DIY Dog Crate Console is the perfect combination of form and function. Forget the retail dog crates and build a piece of furniture that will accommodate two large dog, it has plenty of drawers for storage and the bottom drawer hides the food and water! Free plans by www.shanty-2-chic.com

materials.name: Roll of black PVC coated welded wire

steps.title: Step 1
steps.body: Cut the side pieces to size and drill 1 1/2" pocket holes into the two side boards. Attach the side pieces to the top pieces with 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue. Build two frames.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.body: Cut the mesh to size and attach it to the back of the side assemblies with 3/4" staples.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.body: Cut the front and back frame pieces to size and drill 1 1/2" pocket holes into the ends of the top and bottom apron pieces. Attach the apron pieces to the legs with 2 1/2" pocket holes and wood glue. Build two frames.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.body: Cut the vertical dividers to size and drill 1 1/2" pocket holes in both ends. Attach them to the top and bottom apron pieces with 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue. Do this for both frames.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.body: Cut the horizontal dividers to size and drill 3/4" pocket holes into both ends. Attach them to the vertical dividers with 1 1/4" pocket hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.body: Cut the mesh to size and attach it to the packframe with 3/4" staples.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.body: Attach the side assemblies to the back frame with 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.body: Attach the side assemblies to the back frame with 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.body: Cut the bottom supports to size and drill 3/4" pocket holes into both ends. Attach them to bottom, front and back aprons with 1 1/4" pocket hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.body: Cut the bottom panel to size and drill 3/4" pocket holes around all edges. Slide it on top of the bottom supports and attach it to the bottom and side aprons with 1 1/4" pocket hole screws and wood glue. The bottom panel should be flush with the top of the bottom aprons.

steps.title: Step 11
steps.body: Cut the top support pieces to size and drill 1 1/2" pocket holes into both ends. Attach them to the top aprons with 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Step 12
steps.body: Cut the center dividers to size and drill 3/4" pocket holes around all edges. Attach them tot he vertical dividers with 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Step 13
steps.body: Cut the door pieces to size and drill 1 1/2" pocket holes into the side boards. Attach them to the top and bottom pieces with 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Step 14
steps.body: Cut the mesh to size and atta h it to the back of teh door frames with 3/4" staples.

steps.title: Step 15
steps.body: Cut the top and middle drawer pieces to size and drill 3/4" pocket holes into the ends of the long pieces. Attach the long pieces to the short pieces with 1 1/4" pocket hole screws and wood glue. Build two drawers.

steps.title: Step 16
steps.body: Cut the drawer bottoms to size and attach them to the bottom of the drawer frame with 3/4" staples and wood glue.

steps.title: Step 17
steps.body: Cut the bowl drawer pieces to size. Drill 3/4" pocket holes into both ends of the long pieces and attach them to teh short pieces with 1 1/4" pocket hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Step 18
steps.body: Cut the top piece of the dog bowl to size and use a jigsaw to cut the circles for the bowls. Attach the top inside the frame and use 1 1/4" brad nails and wood glue to attach. The top should be flush with the top of the frame.

steps.title: Step 19
steps.body: Install the drawers inside the cabinet with drawer slides. You can get more details on how we installed ours in the post above!

steps.title: Step 20
steps.body: Cut the drawer face pieces to size and attach them to the front of the drawrs with 1 1/4" wood screws, from the inside of the drawer and into the back of the drawer face.

steps.title: Step 21
steps.body: Cut the top panel to size then, measure and cut the side trim pieces to size. Attach the side trim pieces with wood glue and 1" pin nails.

steps.title: Step 22
steps.body: Measure and cut the front and back trim pieces to size. Attach them to the top panel with 1" pin nails and wood glue. To attach the top, use 1 3/4" wood screws from the top supports and into the plywood top.

steps.title: Step 23
steps.body: Now, just add a finish, hardware and dog bowls and you're done! Make sure to share your builds with us on social media and use #shanty2chic so we can share them too! Happy Building!

steps.title: Step 24
steps.body: Now, just add a finish, hardware and dog bowls and you're done! Make sure to share your builds with us on social media and use #shanty2chic so we can share them too! Happy Building!
